patch.value:
# PM CLI Dogfood Audit Results (2026-04-26)

## Executive Summary
The pm CLI is a mature, highly extensible project management tool. Core functionality is solid with 1064/1065 tests passing. The telemetry pipeline is fully operational. Calendar, extensions, and SDK are functional with specific improvement areas identified.

## Test Results
- 78/79 test files pass
- 1064/1065 individual tests pass
- 1 test fixed during audit (health check assertion)

## Areas Verified Working
1. Core CRUD lifecycle (all 6 item types)
2. Hierarchy (parent/child linking, deps tree)
3. Claim/release/start-task/pause-task/close-task workflow
4. Comments/notes/learnings append-style updates
5. File/doc/test linking
6. History with deterministic hash chains
7. Calendar (4 views x 3 formats)
8. Extensions (install/manage/doctor/activate)
9. Telemetry (9-container stack, E2E event flow)
10. Search (hybrid mode with fallback)
11. Aggregate, dedupe-audit, validate, health, stats
12. Config, governance presets
13. Completion (bash/zsh/fish)
14. Update-many with dry-run
15. Context command for agent triage

## Issues Found (filed as pm items)
1. pm-hb6x: Integration test health check assertion missing telemetry (P0, fixed)
2. pm-bhhe: Aggregate group-by limited to parent|type only (P2)
3. Calendar --full-period rejected for agenda view (P2)
4. pm-6y6i: Templates command syntax unclear (P2)
5. pm-qrxb: SDK docs missing cli-contracts layer documentation (P1)
6. Update-many error message lacks guidance on available flags (P3)

## Extensibility Assessment
- Extension API: 14 register methods + 5 hooks = comprehensive
- 9 capabilities: commands, renderers, hooks, schema, importers, search, parser, preflight, services
- SDK exports proper types for all extension interfaces
- Starter extension example exists
- Bundled extensions (beads, todos) work correctly

## Agent UX Assessment
- TOON output is token-efficient and structured
- JSON output is machine-parseable
- Error messages follow consistent pattern with context
- Context command provides excellent triage snapshot
- Contracts command enables programmatic flag discovery
- Completion generation works for bash/zsh/fish

## Telemetry Security
- .gitignore excludes runtime/telemetry/ and .env
- Sensitive data redaction in place (emails, tokens, passwords, paths)
- No hardcoded secrets in source
- Operations docs stored in gitignored .agents/pm/search/

## Recommendations
1. Expand aggregate group-by fields for universal PM use
2. Document SDK cli-contracts layer
3. Add capability requirement docs to EXTENSIONS.md
4. Export ExtensionCommandArgumentDefinition from SDK
5. Add @deprecated on CommandDefinition.handler
6. Clarify templates command syntax
2026-04-26 Comprehensive PM CLI Dogfood Audit - Full Results

Complete manual dogfood audit of the pm CLI covering core commands, SDK, extensions, calendar, telemetry, agent UX, and extensibility. Conducted in a temporary sandbox project exercising all command groups.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Outcome mapping 2026-04-27: all six audit findings recorded in this decision are now terminal -> pm-hb6x (closed), pm-bhhe (closed), pm-8qpc (closed), pm-6y6i (closed), pm-qrxb (closed), pm-twtu (closed). Additional dogfood follow-up items from continuation wave are also closed: pm-p0ij, pm-a4z3, pm-cvwi, with parent epics pm-r15d/pm-lnq3/pm-5rjn reconciled and closed.

## Executive Summary

## Test Results
- 78/79 test files pass

7. Calendar (4 views x 3 formats)

9. Telemetry (9-container stack, E2E event flow)
10. Search (hybrid mode with fallback)
11. Aggregate, dedupe-audit, validate, health, stats
12. Config, governance presets

## Issues Found (filed as pm items)
1. pm-hb6x: Integration test health check assertion missing telemetry (P0, fixed)
2. pm-bhhe: Aggregate group-by limited to parent|type only (P2)
3. Calendar --full-period rejected for agenda view (P2)
4. pm-6y6i: Templates command syntax unclear (P2)
5. pm-qrxb: SDK docs missing cli-contracts layer documentation (P1)
6. Update-many error message lacks guidance on available flags (P3)

## Extensibility Assessment
- Extension API: 14 register methods + 5 hooks = comprehensive
- 9 capabilities: commands, renderers, hooks, schema, importers, search, parser, preflight, services

- Starter extension example exists
- Bundled extensions (beads, todos) work correctly

- TOON output is token-efficient and structured
- JSON output is machine-parseable
- Error messages follow consistent pattern with context

## Telemetry Security

- Sensitive data redaction in place (emails, tokens, passwords, paths)
- No hardcoded secrets in source
- Operations docs stored in gitignored .agents/pm/search/

## Recommendations
1. Expand aggregate group-by fields for universal PM use
2. Document SDK cli-contracts layer
3. Add capability requirement docs to EXTENSIONS.md
4. Export ExtensionCommandArgumentDefinition from SDK
5. Add @deprecated on CommandDefinition.handler
6. Clarify templates command syntax


## 2026-04-27 disposition
Audit findings have been mapped to tracked issues/features and are now closed in tracker state. No active follow-up items remain from the original six finding buckets. Future dogfood deltas should be filed as net-new children under current governance/telemetry lineage as needed.
Comprehensive dogfood audit outcomes were reconciled to tracked implementation items, and all mapped findings are now closed with evidence.
Decision record links each audit finding to a tracked item state and closes once no mapped findings remain active.
All mapped findings (pm-hb6x, pm-bhhe, pm-8qpc, pm-6y6i, pm-qrxb, pm-twtu) are closed; continuation follow-ups are closed as well, so the decision outcome is complete.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Audit findings disposition mapped and fully closed in tracker lifecycle.

message: Close: pm-z87r disposition completed with all mapped findings terminal.

value.text: Final handoff 2026-04-27: decision closure verified by final gate -> pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift --json (warn limited to historical rows outside this closure wave), pm normalize --dry-run --json (closed_resolution_backfill count=10), pm context --limit 20 (active_items=0).
